confirmed.title: hooks.md says '10 reusable hooks' but 11 exist; import list omits useResourceUpdates
confirmed.suggested_fix: Change '10 reusable hooks' to '11 reusable hooks' and add useResourceUpdates to the import example on lines 6-9.
confirmed.detail: Line 3 states '10 reusable hooks. 11 tree-shakeable subpath entry points.' and the import block (lines 6-9) lists 10 hook names. The package actually exports 11 hooks from packages-js/hooks/src/index.ts (useAction, useArqelForm, useBreakpoint, useCanAccess, useFieldDependencies, useFlash, useNavigation, useArqelOptimistic, useResource, useResourceUpdates, useTable) and package.json declares 11 subpath entry points. useResourceUpdates is missing from both the count and the import example. The sibling SKILL.md (packages-js/hooks/SKILL.md:13) correctly lists all 11 and says '11 entry points subpath'.
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides on disk. apps/docs/reference/typescript/hooks.md:3 states "10 reusable hooks. 11 tree-shakeable subpath entry points." and the import block (lines 6-8) lists exactly 10 hook names, omitting useResourceUpdates. The real source packages-js/hooks/src/index.ts exports 11 hooks: useAction, useArqelForm, useBreakpoint, useCanAccess, useFieldDependencies, useFlash, useNavigation, useArqelOptimistic, useResource, useResourceUpdates, useTable. package.json declares 11 subpath entry points including ./useResourceUpdates. So the "10 reusable hooks" count is objectively false (it's 11), the doc is internally inconsistent (claims 11 entry points but 10 hooks), and the import example omits useResourceUpdates. The doc is in-scope (apps/docs reference, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated) and not present in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json.

confirmed.title: actions.md lists constant VARIANT_FILLED which does not exist (actual is VARIANT_DEFAULT)
confirmed.suggested_fix: Replace 'VARIANT_FILLED / OUTLINE / GHOST' with 'VARIANT_DEFAULT / OUTLINE / GHOST / DESTRUCTIVE' to match Action.php.
confirmed.detail: Line 13 documents the typed constants as 'VARIANT_FILLED / OUTLINE / GHOST'. packages/actions/src/Action.php defines public const string VARIANT_DEFAULT='default' (line 48), VARIANT_OUTLINE (50), VARIANT_GHOST (52), VARIANT_DESTRUCTIVE (54). There is no VARIANT_FILLED constant; the default variant constant is VARIANT_DEFAULT, and VARIANT_DESTRUCTIVE is omitted from the doc.
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides on disk. apps/docs/reference/php/actions.md:13 documents typed constants as 'VARIANT_FILLED / OUTLINE / GHOST'. packages/actions/src/Action.php defines no VARIANT_FILLED constant; it has VARIANT_DEFAULT='default' (line 48, also the $variant default at line 71), VARIANT_OUTLINE (50), VARIANT_GHOST (52), VARIANT_DESTRUCTIVE (54). The doc's 'VARIANT_FILLED' is objectively false (no such constant), and it misrepresents the default constant name (DEFAULT, not FILLED) while omitting VARIANT_DESTRUCTIVE. The doc is in-scope (apps/docs, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated) and the claim is not present in the docs-fixed-ledger.json.

confirmed.title: types.md mischaracterizes FieldType as a discriminated object union with props
confirmed.suggested_fix: Document FieldType as a string-literal union of 21 type tags, and document FieldSchema as the (non-generic) discriminated union of FieldBase<TType, TProps> members. Remove the fabricated 'FieldSchema<T extends FieldType = FieldType> = {...} & T' generic.
confirmed.detail: Lines 16-24 describe FieldType as 'a discriminated union over type' shaped like '{ type: "text"; props: TextFieldProps } | ... 18 more', and line 29 shows 'type FieldSchema<T extends FieldType = FieldType> = { ... } & T'. In packages-js/types/src/fields.ts, FieldType (line 8) is a plain 21-member string-literal union ('text' | 'textarea' | ... | 'hidden'). The discriminated object union is FieldSchema (src/fields.ts, union of FieldBase<'text', TextFieldProps> | ...), which is NON-generic — not 'FieldSchema<T> = {...} & T'. The doc swaps the two concepts.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides against disk. apps/docs/reference/typescript/types.md lines 16-24 describe FieldType as a "discriminated union over type" shaped like '{ type: "text"; props: TextFieldProps } | ... 18 more'. But packages-js/types/src/fields.ts line 8 defines FieldType as a plain 21-member string-literal union ('text' | 'textarea' | ... | 'hidden') — no objects, no props. The actual discriminated object union is FieldSchema (fields.ts line 211: TextFieldSchema | ... where each = FieldBase<'text', TextFieldProps>), and it is NON-generic. The doc line 29 shows 'type FieldSchema<T extends FieldType = FieldType> = { ... } & T', which is false: FieldSchema takes no type parameter and is a union of FieldBase<...> members, not an object intersected with & T. The doc objectively swaps/mischaracterizes the two types. File is in-scope (docs reference, not PLANNING/generated/CHANGELOG). Not present in docs-fixed-ledger.json.

confirmed.title: types.md isFieldType / resolveFieldEntry signatures do not match source
confirmed.suggested_fix: Update isFieldType to '<T extends FieldType>(field: FieldSchema, type: T): field is Extract<FieldSchema, { type: T }>' and change resolveFieldEntry return type from 'FieldSchema | undefined' to 'FieldSchema | null'.
confirmed.detail: Line 51 documents 'isFieldType<T extends FieldType["type"]>(field, type): field is FieldSchema<Extract<FieldType, { type: T }>>'. Actual (src/fields.ts:236): 'isFieldType<T extends FieldType>(field: FieldSchema, type: T): field is Extract<FieldSchema, { type: T }>' — FieldType has no ['type'] index (it is a string union) and the result is Extract<FieldSchema,...>, not a generic FieldSchema<...>. Line 54 documents 'resolveFieldEntry(...): FieldSchema | undefined' but src/forms.ts:124 returns 'FieldSchema | null'.
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides on disk. Doc apps/docs/reference/typescript/types.md (in-scope, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG, not in ledger) line 51 documents `isFieldType<T extends FieldType['type']>(field: FieldSchema, type: T): field is FieldSchema<Extract<FieldType, { type: T }>>`. Actual source packages-js/types/src/fields.ts:236-241 is `isFieldType<T extends FieldType>(field: FieldSchema, type: T): field is Extract<FieldSchema, { type: T }>`. FieldType (fields.ts:8) is a plain string union ('text' | 'textarea' | ...), so `FieldType['type']` is not a valid index, and the return guard is `Extract<FieldSchema, {type: T}>`, not a generic `FieldSchema<...>` (FieldSchema is not parameterized that way here). Doc line 54 documents `resolveFieldEntry(...): FieldSchema | undefined` but source forms.ts:124 returns `FieldSchema | null`. Both claims objectively contradict current code. Note: cited line numbers fields.ts:236 and forms.ts:124 matched exactly; finding's path 'src/fields.ts' resolves to packages-js/types/src/fields.ts.

confirmed.suggested_fix: Either remove the '## `useTheme()`' section from the @arqel-dev/hooks reference, or change it to a one-line cross-reference like 'For theme state use `useTheme()` from `@arqel-dev/react` (or `@arqel-dev/theme`) — it is not part of `@arqel-dev/hooks`.' Apply the same change to the pt-BR and es mirrors at line 104.
confirmed.detail: The page is titled '# `@arqel-dev/hooks` — API Reference', states '10 reusable hooks', and its import example (lines 6-8) lists exactly 10 hooks WITHOUT useTheme. Yet line 104 adds a top-level '## `useTheme()`' section presenting it as a hooks-package API. Proof: `@arqel-dev/hooks` does not export or re-export useTheme — it is absent from packages-js/hooks/src/ (the exported hooks are useAction, useArqelForm, useArqelOptimistic, useBreakpoint, useCanAccess, useFieldDependencies, useFlash, useNavigation, useOptimistic, useResource, useResourceUpdates, useTable), and packages-js/hooks/package.json declares no @arqel-dev/theme dependency. The actual useTheme implementations live in @arqel-dev/react (packages-js/react/src/providers/ThemeProvider.tsx, re-exported via providers/index.ts) and @arqel-dev/theme (packages-js/theme/src/useTheme.ts). So `import { useTheme } from '@arqel-dev/hooks'` fails. The section's hedge ('Re-exported from @arqel-dev/theme') also misattributes provenance, since the version surfaced by ArqelProvider/createArqelApp is @arqel-dev/react's. Mirrored verbatim at the same line 104 in apps/docs/pt-BR/reference/typescript/hooks.md and apps/docs/es/reference/typescript/hooks.md.
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides on disk. Doc apps/docs/reference/typescript/hooks.md is in-scope (not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG) and not in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json (grep for usetheme/hooks.md returned nothing). The page is titled '# `@arqel-dev/hooks` — API Reference', line 3 says '10 reusable hooks', and the import example (lines 6-8) lists exactly 10 hooks WITHOUT useTheme. Line 104 adds '## `useTheme()`' presenting it as a hooks-package API ('Re-exported from @arqel-dev/theme'). The actual source contradicts this: packages-js/hooks/src/index.ts (the package entry) exports no useTheme — only useAction, useArqelForm, useBreakpoint, useCanAccess, useFieldDependencies, useFlash, useNavigation, useArqelOptimistic, useResource, useResourceUpdates, useTable. package.json exports map has no ./useTheme subpath and declares no @arqel-dev/theme dependency (only @arqel-dev/types dep, @arqel-dev/react peer). grep for useTheme across packages-js/hooks/ returns nothing. Therefore `import { useTheme } from '@arqel-dev/hooks'` fails — the doc claim is objectively false/misleading. Same line 104 mirrored in pt-BR and es variants.
